description_long: This temple is a stepped, pyramidal structure reminiscent of Mesoamerican architecture, constructed from large, tan stone blocks. The building features multiple receding tiers, each with broad, sloped walls and geometric paneling, giving it a monumental and symmetrical appearance. A wide central staircase ascends from the ground to the uppermost platform, flanked by stone balustrades. The roof is flat at each tier, culminating in a small, raised central chamber or shrine at the top. The temple sits on a grassy base, with stone pathways and retaining walls outlining its perimeter. Decorative elements are minimal, focusing on geometric patterns and the imposing mass of the stonework.
description: A stepped, stone pyramid temple with broad terraces, geometric paneling, and a central staircase leading to a small shrine at the summit.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide central staircase, symmetrical terraces, and the main entrance at the base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the stepped terraces, geometric wall patterns, and the staircase extending along the left side.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back terraces and the small central shrine at the top, with no visible staircase.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the right-side staircase and the layered, receding terraces.
attribute_ornamentation: minimalist geometric patterns, low-relief stone paneling
attribute_entrance_design: broad central staircase with stone balustrades leading to a rectangular entryway at the base, unadorned and monumental
